goal: Distinguish true trajectory-specific groove learning from smoothing, momentum, or routing artifacts.

core_hypothesis.claim: The platform learns wave trajectories by deepening identity-specific continuation grooves.
must_show: same trajectory becomes easier to continue
different trajectories do not benefit from the wrong groove
previously learned grooves survive later training
groove helps when external input is partially removed
noise does not falsely create strong grooves

test_battery.purpose: Prove groove deepening across repeated traversal.
success: same groove_id dominates across A passes
continuation_mismatch_mean trend decreases
replay_A < A_pass1
trace_feedback_gain or successful_traversals increases

test_battery.purpose: Prove groove is directional, not generic smoothing.
success: B mismatch > trained_A mismatch
B routes to different groove or fails to lock to A groove
A_after_B returns to A groove
A_after_B mismatch remains near trained_A mismatch

test_battery.purpose: Test whether training B damages learned A.
success: replay_A uses original A groove
replay_A mismatch does not degrade more than 10-15%
replay_B uses B groove
A and B groove_ids remain distinct

test_battery.purpose: Test continuation when external wave is reduced or removed.
success: internal continuation remains aligned for a measurable horizon
continuation_mismatch rises gradually, not immediately
trained_A continuation beats untrained baseline
noise does not create stable high-confidence groove
A still forms its own groove
noise groove, if created, has low success_count
adaptive threshold rejects unstable trace segments

test_battery.purpose: Test undesired-event detection as loss of continuation alignment.
success: continuation_mismatch rises before or at transition
groove_id instability increases near transition
operator sequence changes before event segment dominates

acceptance_thresholds.learning_curve: replay_A mismatch at least 10% lower than A_pass1 average across repeats
acceptance_thresholds.directionality: B mismatch at least 10% higher than trained_A when B is untrained
acceptance_thresholds.forgetting: A replay after B training degrades no more than 15%
acceptance_thresholds.withheld_continuation: trained continuation beats no-memory baseline by at least 10%
acceptance_thresholds.noise_control: noise must not produce a dominant stable groove with high successful_traversals

final_instruction: Run the full test battery with repeats and baselines. Do not claim robust learning unless A improves, B separates, A survives B training, and withheld continuation beats baseline.
